surviving_findings.summary: README.md (declared single entry point) never links operator-playbook.md — the document defining the lifecycle regiment (§1-§7). Reachable only via SKILL.md's reference map (a model-facing document). Found independently by BOTH walkers via executed grep/link-chase; a newcomer following 'README + explicit links' can bootstrap but never learns §3 build ritual, handoff-is-a-claim, or when to order /external-audit. README quick-start also covers only the new-project scenario — §4 (existing project) is unreachable by explicit link.
surviving_findings.playbook_ref: README.md vs operator-playbook.md header; README → operator-playbook discovery chain

surviving_findings.summary: Approval-gate contradiction: bootstrap-checklist Phase 1 says 'present this and get approval before writing'; playbook §1 (the operator's script) describes bootstrap as fire-and-forget with no approval step. In a headless/one-command flow the agent must stall or self-approve — the regiment doesn't say which. Walker self-approved to proceed.

Phase 5 kit doesn't say who seeds the feature ledger or to what granularity; §2 promises 'получишь features.json' but decomposition of the product into F1-F3 with verify contracts was the agent's unilateral invention with no prescribed operator review of the seeded ledger.

Even with the 'explicit contracts' rule, feature #1 forced a silent micro-decision: nothing covers how tests import a src/-layout package with no pyproject.toml; agent invented conftest.py + sys.path.insert — exactly the per-session invention class the checklist warns about.

Per-tool resolve guidance (.venv first, PATH fallback) has a PATH-hygiene hole: in the new project init.sh's fallback resolved python3 from a DIFFERENT project's venv inherited via PATH. Oracle green but the interpreter is foreign to the project; regiment never says to check what the fallback actually resolved to. Reproduced live.

Bootstrap Phase 7 verification check has a vague pass criterion: 'claude --print "ls" # settings load + obey' — an ls answer proves nothing about settings; the inner session even asserted '.claude/ ещё нет' (false). Check 'passed' mechanically but a newcomer cannot tell what output would constitute failure. Contrast: §1 stack check has a crisp criterion and passed cleanly.

Phase 0 command list mis-fires on its canonical target: on a zero-commit repo `git log --oneline | wc -l` prints a fatal error to stderr — the first command output a newcomer sees on the empty/new-repo path. Cosmetic, reproduced live.

Gap-report template is split from the audit checklist: audit-checklist.md says 'Output uses the report template in SKILL.md' without duplicating it; a newcomer routed by playbook §4 directly to the checklist must open a third file the playbook never mentions.

external-audit Step 0.1 unconditionally prescribes reading .claude/features.json and .claude/progress/ — in a legacy project without the Phase 5 kit they don't exist and behavior is unspecified ($ARGUMENTS saves the spec, but Step 0.3's preconditions source silently disappears). Newcomer improvises 'absent = no preconditions'. Verified live (ls on fixture).

ROLE_DIR fallback resolution ('first existing path via ls') checks directory existence, not role-file presence. Real fixture case: target project's .claude/agents/ exists containing only a foreign orchestrator.md — with paths 1-2 unavailable, ROLE_DIR would resolve to a role-less directory, the 'no path exists' stop condition would not fire, and subagents would be told to read nonexistent files. Verified live against fixture.

Pre-flight check of native visibility of the 3 agent types is unavailable to a newcomer outside a session: CLI `claude agents` lists sessions not types; types are visible only via interactive /agents or from inside the adjudicator session. The working indirect check (`claude plugin list` → loaded) is nowhere suggested as a pre-flight.

D-cycle trigger '≥5 substantive observations in harness-journal.md' depends on the opt-in journal (default off per playbook §2.3) — for an existing project that never ordered the journal, half the trigger is dead by design; harness-evolution.md doesn't address the legacy scenario. Milestone trigger and strip procedure are executable (--safe-mode and plugin tag/validate confirmed in binary).

verdict.summary: Оба наивных исполнителя прошли регламент до конца с вердиктом passed_with_friction и реальным исполнением шагов (живые репо, claude --print, RED→GREEN коммиты, CLI-проверки бинаря) — ни одного blocker'а, регламент проходим новичком. Ни одно finding не опровергнуто исполнением другого walker'а (dismissed: 0); ключевой gap — недостижимость operator-playbook.md от заявленной точки входа README — найден обоими независимо и поднят до corroborated major. Выживают 2 major'а (README→playbook discovery chain; противоречие approval-gate между playbook §1 и bootstrap-checklist Phase 1) и 10 minor'ов — каждый actionable item для D-цикла Phase 4. Замечание по rule 3: legacy-walker исполнил §4 и §5 (Шаг 0 + резолвинг ролей) против живой фикстуры, но полный 3-ролевой прогон §5 не запускал, а §6/§7 прошёл как чтение + проверки существования инструментов — доверие к его выводам по §6/§7 чуть ниже, хотя и они опираются на исполненные ls/claude --help/claude plugin, а не на чистое чтение. Итог: confirmed_with_debt — регламент работает end-to-end, но долг по discoverability и недоспецифицированным веткам (headless approval, legacy без Phase 5 kit, ROLE_DIR edge-case) требует фолда в Phase 4.
